mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital spending growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces rate hike headwinds, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day returns in > 50% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in > 55% of quarters over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold but 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales alongside rising inflation indicates demand destruction under price pressures, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint pattern coincides with positive 5-day returns in > 50% of observations during stagflationary periods.
